evals.prompt: I have 20,000 news articles and want to discover the main themes with topic modeling.
evals.expected_output: Invokes alterlab-text-as-data: recommends BERTopic (or LDA) for discovery, chooses K by coherence and interpretability, and validates topic stability across seeds rather than trusting a single run.

assertions.value: Selects a topic model for discovery and validates topic reliability across runs, not one stochastic solution.

evals.prompt: I want to measure how much populist rhetoric each speech contains using a validated word list.
evals.expected_output: Invokes alterlab-text-as-data: uses a dictionary/lexicon method for measurement, normalizes by length, and validates the score against human coding — treating the lexicon as an instrument.

assertions.value: Uses a dictionary/lexicon measurement approach and requires validation against human coding.

evals.prompt: Embed my documents so I can cluster them and find which ones are most similar.
evals.expected_output: Invokes alterlab-text-as-data: uses sentence-transformers embeddings (e.g. all-MiniLM-L6-v2) and cosine similarity / clustering, noting the embedding model should suit the domain and language.

evals.prompt: I want to fine-tune a BERT model on my labeled dataset to build a custom classifier.
evals.expected_output: Should NOT trigger this skill; defers to alterlab-transformers. Fine-tuning/training a transformer model is that skill's domain, not corpus measurement.

evals.prompt: I want to do a close reading and manual annotation of a small set of literary texts for a humanities project.
evals.expected_output: Should NOT trigger this skill; defers to alterlab-digital-humanities. Interpretive close-reading/annotation is not quantitative text-as-data measurement.
